# Sterling architecture & extension document (generated)

Sterling documents and extends itself by the same registry + checked-contract +
fail-loud discipline it imposes (§15). For every extensible set below: the
registry is the single source of truth, the named checks guard it, and the
"extending this" guidance is quoted from the set's owning feature article —
the durable store is authoritative; this file is a projection of it.

## Extensible set: signals — Signals (the closed exit enum)

- Registry: packages/schemas/src/transient.ts — SIGNALS + SIGNAL_PAYLOADS (consumed by the brain REACTIONS table)
- Checks: scripts/check-totality.mjs (npm run check) + the brain totality test (packages/mcp-server brain.test)

**Extending this:** The brain decides, the conductor executes (§5.2): every possible signal has exactly one wired reaction, unknown signals are unrepresentable rather than mishandled, and loops convert to judgment escalations at configured caps instead of running away. Pure function → trivially testable, no store access, no LLM.

Full article: `brain-signal-protocol` (v2, 2026-07-12T17:25:30.317Z) — Articles tab or `knowledge_query`.

## Extensible set: record-types — Record types (durable knowledge shapes)

- Registry: packages/schemas/src/records.ts — RECORD_TYPES (schema + fts + fileKeys per member) + SYSTEM_REASONS/DRAIN_VERBS
- Checks: schemas registry test (1:1, unregistered rejected) + store write validation on every create

**Extending this:** One definition per shape, consumed by both the MCP server and the TUI (invariant 1); no raw path ever enters the store (invariant 2); for every extensible set the registry + its consistency check exist before members are added (invariant 3). Adding a record type or maintenance lane is a registry-member addition here, never a parallel definition elsewhere. Since 2026-07-17 (decision a0fc8743): featureArticleSchema + referenceMaterialSchema carry an optional working_tree symbolic name and config.ts a working_trees name→path map — detached-copy records resolve their file paths against their own tree, keeping machine-specific paths in config and repo-relative file_keys in records (invariant 2 holds).

Full article: `record-schemas-registry` (v18, 2026-07-17T10:04:33.964Z) — Articles tab or `knowledge_query`.

## Extensible set: agents — Agents (the roster)

- Registry: agent-templates/registry.json (1:1 with templates)
- Checks: scripts/check-agent-registry.mjs, check-agent-prompts.mjs (§7.3 sections), check-spawn-contracts.mjs (§7.4), check-agents-visible.mjs (runtime)

**Extending this:** The roster is the §7.1 division of judgment: each agent owns exactly one kind of decision, receives a declared input set, and exits through the closed signal enum; skills are SOPs the conductor executes, not prose suggestions. Quality is enforced by the linters (seven prompt sections, spawn contracts, skill references), not by review.

Full article: `agent-roster-and-skills` (v21, 2026-07-17T09:25:41.649Z) — Articles tab or `knowledge_query`.

## Extensible set: hooks — Hooks (conduct enforcement at platform seams)

- Registry: hooks/hooks.json (project-level registrations) + agent-template frontmatter (per-agent hooks)
- Checks: hooks.json emission check (forward-slash commands) + scripts/tests/hooks-full.test.mjs / enforcement.test.mjs

**Extending this:** Checkable rules are hooks, not prose (§6): every gate fires at the platform seam it guards, blocking gates fail closed, soft gates degrade loud through check_skipped — and because hooks are bundled standalone, the standalone launch path has nothing to repair. Hook-semantics drift can weaken a backstop, never a gate (the disposal gate lives in the script, not the hook). Since 2026-07-17 (decision a0fc8743): H7's ownership join EXCLUDES records declaring a working_tree — a root-session touch of a path that a detached-copy article happens to share never mints that article a reconcile item.

Full article: `hooks-suite` (v35, 2026-07-17T10:04:59.903Z) — Articles tab or `knowledge_query`.

## Extensible set: tools — Tools (the §10 MCP surface)

- Registry: packages/mcp-server/src/server.ts — registerTool set (the one write path to the store; H15 guards shell bypass)
- Checks: server.test.ts exact tool-list assertion + zod input schemas per tool

**Extending this:** Coarse tools are safe because schemas are exact — every write revalidates at the store; protocol violations throw and return in-band so spawned agents see the message and self-correct (§5.2). Reads are where staleness becomes visible: no sweeps, no background jobs, every flag computed at the moment someone actually consumes the record — and since 2026-07-17 (decision a0fc8743, comsoft-juiced incident) the two file-drift wires and the baseline hashing resolve each record against ITS declared working tree (working_tree → config.working_trees), abstaining LOUD (verify_before_use, no enqueue, no fabricated baselines) on an unmapped tree name — a record describing a detached copy is never checked against the project root's same-named files.

Full article: `mcp-tool-surface` (v22, 2026-07-17T10:04:19.925Z) — Articles tab or `knowledge_query`.

## Extensible set: toolchain-adapters — Toolchain adapters (per-stack capability layer)

- Registry: scripts/adapters/registry.json — name → module, fixed interface (capabilities/testPathGlobs/runCommands/runTests)
- Checks: checkAdapterRegistry() via scripts/check-agent-registry.mjs + adapter tests in scripts/tests/enforcement.test.mjs

**Extending this:** One classification authority per stack, declared not improvised: a new toolchain joins by registering an adapter module that exports the fixed interface, and every consuming check, hook glob, and agent allowlist derives from its declarations. A check whose required capability is absent degrades loud (check_skipped listed at the merge gate), never silently green (P5). Init warns once per absent optional capability at declaration time. TypeScript package tests are first-class: built from each owning package's tsconfig and run from dist (Node16 .js specifiers don't resolve from src), so a TS red/green check classifies on real assertions, never a false load-crash.

Full article: `toolchain-adapters` (v9, 2026-07-12T11:36:46.504Z) — Articles tab or `knowledge_query`.
